MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Designate Rebuttal Expert Witness
Off Calendar Aug-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF EDUARDO PANIAGUA's Motion To Designate Rebuttal Expert Witness.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's Notice of Motion and Motion to Designate Rebuttal Expert Witness is DENIED. Plaintiff moves for relief under CCP Sec. 2034.230, 2034.280, 2034.310, and 2034.720. None of these statutes appear to apply to the relief requested by Plaintiff. 2034.720 is titled "Scope of judicial authority to grant tardy filing of list; satisfaction of certain conditions." Per 2034.710, this subsection applies to "any party who has failed to submit expert witness information on the date specified in a demand. Here, Plaintiff timely disclosed his expert and does not seek to serve a late disclosure. (See Defendants' evidence, Ex. 5.) It appears that Plaintiff is seeking relief under 2034.620 ("Expert witness lists; court authority upon satisfaction of certain conditions"). That statute allows the Court to "grant leave to augment or amend an expert witness list" if certain conditions are met. Regardless, both Sec. 2034.720 and 2034.620 require that the moving party both (1) move promptly for relief, and (2) serve a copy of the proposed expert witness information. Here, Plaintiff failed to move promptly for relief or serve a copy of the proposed expert witness information on all parties. Plaintiff argues that he became aware of the need to augment his expert list at the deposition of Ms. Randich on May 23, 2023. Plaintiff filed this Motion on July 5, 2012, some 43 days later. No proposed expert witness information has been provided. Finally, decisions on whether Ms. Randich offered testimony outside the scope of her designation and whether her testimony impermissibly includes "ultimate issues" lies with the trial judge.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal
Off Calendar May-24-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS EDUARDO PANIAGUA, ELENA ASTURIAS' Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiffs' motion for attorney's fees on appeal is denied.

The general rule is that "fees under [Civil Code] section 1717 are awarded to the party who prevailed on the contract overall, not to a party who prevailed only at an interim procedural step." (DisputeSuite.com, LLC v. Scoreinc.com (2017) 2 Cal.5th 968, 977; accord, City of West Hollywood v. Khiagi (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 739, 753.) In DisputeSuite.com, the California Supreme Court discussed with approval several cases in which "a party has won a procedural victory, ending the contract litigation in one court but the litigation has continued or could continue in a different forum." (Id. at 975-977.) Those cases stand for the principle that "[a] party does not become the prevailing party under the statute merely by obtaining a forum for resolution of the contractual dispute or by moving it from one forum to another." (Id. at 976.) That principle is controlling here. Plaintiffs misread the prevailing party fees clause of the Payoff Agreement. A petition to compel arbitration is not a "provisional remedy in aid of arbitration." (See Code Civ. Proc. 1281.8 [defining provisional remedies]. Unlike the fees clause in Acosta v. Kerrigan (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1124, the fees clause in the Payoff Agreement does not authorize an interim award of attorney's fees in connection with plaintiffs' successful opposition to defendants' motion to compel arbitration. (See Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown, Inc. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 515, 525.).

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEFENDANT MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC, BEAR BRUIN VENTURES, INC., WILLIAM STUART, CAROLYN STUART'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is off calendar. On March 14, 2019, Defendants appealed this court's order that denied their motion to compel arbitration. That appeal divests this court of jurisdiction per Code of Civil Procedure 916. "[A]n appeal from the denial of a motion to compel arbitration automatically stays all further trial court proceedings on the merits." (Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 190.)
